description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a camouflage pattern of green, brown, and gray. The helicopter features a large main rotor with four long, black blades and a smaller tail rotor at the rear. The cockpit is equipped with multiple tinted windows, providing wide visibility for the crew, and the nose is fitted with sensors or targeting equipment. The landing gear consists of robust skids, reinforced with crossbars for stability during ground contact. Markings, including the alphanumeric code 'H 246', are visible on the side of the fuselage. The overall design suggests a focus on tactical operations and durability in urban or rugged environments.
description: A camouflaged military helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, tinted cockpit windows, and sturdy landing skids, marked with 'H 246' on its side.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, camouflage paint, cockpit windows, and the 'H 246' marking.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail rotor, landing skids, and the camouflage pattern, with the 'H 246' marking visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids beneath the fuselage.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, colored black with no visible markings, each blade extending nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor with four blades, mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored black and partially enclosed by a protective structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular landing skids made of metal, painted dark gray or black, reinforced with crossbars for stability and support.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows with a dark tint, arranged in a wraparound configuration at the front and sides, framed by the fuselage with no visible markings.
